summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the V1 Files API download endpoint is vulnerable to path traversal. An authenticated user (obtained via the auto_login endpoint) can read arbitrary files from the server by supplying ../ sequences in the file_name URL parameter.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should cause the path traversal exploit to read /etc/passwd from the server, demonstrating that the file_name parameter escapes the intended storage directory and can access any file on the filesystem.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned the target repository, built the Langflow server, obtained a superuser JWT via the unauthenticated auto_login endpoint, created a flow, and successfully read /etc/passwd via the path traversal GET /api/v1/files/download/{flow_id}/../../../etc/passwd.
verification_method: The exploit script sends a GET request with ../../../etc/passwd as the file_name parameter. It checks for HTTP 200 status and verifies the response body contains 'root:' which is the expected first field of /etc/passwd entries. This distinguishes a successful path traversal from a generic error or file-not-found response.
evidence_produced_by_run: The auto_login endpoint returned a superuser JWT without authentication.
A flow was created successfully using the superuser JWT.
The path traversal request to GET /api/v1/files/download/{flow_id}/../../../etc/passwd returned HTTP 200 with the contents of /etc/passwd.
The response contained 'root:' confirming successful reading of the system password file.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the main branch, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
